You may obtain a copy of the License at\n9:  *\n10:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n11:  *\n12:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n13:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n14:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n15:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n16:  * limitations under the License.\n17:  */\n18: \n19: package org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing;\n20: \n21: import org.apache.flink.annotation.Internal;\n22: import org.apache.flink.annotation.VisibleForTesting;\n23: import org.apache.flink.api.common.ExecutionConfig;\n24: import org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.SerializerFactory;\n25: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.AggregatingState;\n26: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.AggregatingStateDescriptor;\n27: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.AppendingState;\n28: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.KeyedStateStore;\n29: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ListState;\n30: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ListStateDescriptor;\n31: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.MapState;\n32: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.MapStateDescriptor;\n33: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.MergingState;\n34: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ReducingState;\n35: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ReducingStateDescriptor;\n36: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.State;\n37: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.StateDescriptor;\n38: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ValueState;\n39: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ValueStateDescriptor;\n40: import org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo.TypeInformation;\n41: import org.apache.flink.api.common.typeutils.TypeSerializer;\n42: import org.apache.flink.api.java.functions.KeySelector;\n43: import org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple.Tuple2;\n44: import org.apache.flink.api.java.typeutils.runtime.TupleSerializer;\n45: import org.apache.flink.metrics.Counter;\n46: import org.apache.flink.metrics.MetricGroup;\n47: import org.apache.flink.runtime.state.DefaultKeyedStateStore;\n48: import org.apache.flink.runtime.state.KeyedStateBackend;\n49: import org.apache.flink.runtime.state.VoidNamespace;\n50: import org.apache.flink.runtime.state.VoidNamespaceSerializer;\n51: import org.apache.flink.runtime.state.internal.InternalAppendingState;\n52: import org.apache.flink.runtime.state.internal.InternalListState;\n53: import org.apache.flink.runtime.state.internal.InternalMergingState;\n54: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.graph.StreamConfig;\n55: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.AbstractUdfStreamOperator;\n56: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.InternalTimer;\n57: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.InternalTimerService;\n58: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.OneInputStreamOperator;\n59: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.OperatorAttributes;\n60: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.OperatorAttributesBuilder;\n61: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.Output;\n62: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.TimestampedCollector;\n63: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.Triggerable;\n64: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.GlobalWindows;\n65: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.MergingWindowAssigner;\n66: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.WindowAssigner;\n67: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.Trigger;\n68: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.TriggerResult;\n69: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.Window;\n70: import org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalWindowFunction;\n71: import org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.streamrecord.StreamRecord;\n72: import org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.tasks.ProcessingTimeService;\n73: import org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.tasks.StreamTask;\n74: import org.apache.flink.util.OutputTag;\n75: \n76: import java.util.Collection;\n77: \n78: import static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\n79: import static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n80: \n81: /**\n82:  * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n83:  * Trigger}.\n84:  *\n85:  * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n86:  * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n87:  * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n88:  * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n89:  * WindowAssigner}.\n90:  *\n91:  * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n92:  * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n93:  * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n94:  * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n95:  *\n96:  * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n97:  * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n98:  * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n99:  * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n100:  */\n101: @Internal\n102: public class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n103:         extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n104:         implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n105: \n106:     private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n107: \n108:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n109:     // Configuration values and user functions\n110:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n111: \n112:     protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n113: \n114:     private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n115: \n116:     private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n117: \n118:     private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n119: \n120:     /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n121:     protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n122: \n123:     /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n124:     protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n125: \n126:     /**\n127:      * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n128:      *\n129:      * <ul>\n130:      *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n131:      *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n132:      *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n133:      * </ul>\n134:      */\n135:     protected final long allowedLateness;\n136: \n137:     /**\n138:      * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n139:      * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n140:      * to this.\n141:      */\n142:     protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n143: \n144:     private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n145: \n146:     protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n147: \n148:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n149:     // State that is not checkpointed\n150:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n151: \n152:     /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n153:     private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n154: \n155:     /**\n156:      * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n157:      * state is not mergeable.\n158:      */\n159:     private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n160: \n161:     /** The state that holds the merging window metadata (the sets that describe what is merged). */\n162:     private transient InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsState;\n163: \n164:     /**\n165:      * This is given to the {@code InternalWindowFunction} for emitting elements with a given\n166:      * timestamp.\n167:      */\n168:     protected transient TimestampedCollector<OUT> timestampedCollector;\n169: \n170:     protected transient Context triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n171: \n172:     protected transient WindowContext processContext;\n173: \n174:     protected transient WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext windowAssignerContext;\n175: \n176:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n177:     // State that needs to be checkpointed\n178:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n179: \n180:     protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\n181: \n182:     /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\n183:     public WindowOperator(\n184:             WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n185:             TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n186:             KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n187:             TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n188:             StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n189:             InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n190:             Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n191:             long allowedLateness,\n192:             OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n193: \n194:         super(windowFunction);\n195: \n196:         checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\n197: \n198:         checkArgument(\n199:                 windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\n200:                 \"window state serializer is not properly initialized\");\n201: \n202:         this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\n203:         this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\n204:         this.keySelector = checkNotNull(keySelector);\n205:         this.keySerializer = checkNotNull(keySerializer);\n206:         this.windowStateDescriptor = windowStateDescriptor;\n207:         this.trigger = checkNotNull(trigger);\n208:         this.allowedLateness = allowedLateness;\n209:         this.lateDataOutputTag = lateDataOutputTag;\n210:     }\n211: \n212:     @Override\n213:     public void setup(\n214:             StreamTask<?, ?> containingTask,\n215:             StreamConfig config,\n216:             Output<StreamRecord<OUT>> output) {\n217:         super.setup(containingTask, config, output);\n218:     }\n219: \n220:     @Override\n221:     public void setProcessingTimeService(ProcessingTimeService processingTimeService) {\n222:         super.setProcessingTimeService(processingTimeService);\n223:     }\n224: \n225:     @Override\n226:     public void open() throws Exception {\n227:         super.open();\n228: \n229:         this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\n230:         timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\n231: \n232:         internalTimerService = getInternalTimerService(\"window-timers\", windowSerializer, this);\n233: \n234:         triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n235:         processContext = new WindowContext(null);\n236: \n237:         windowAssignerContext =\n238:                 new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n239:                     @Override\n240:                     public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n241:                         return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n242:                     }\n243:                 };\n244: \n245:         // create (or restore) the state that hold the actual window contents\n246:         // NOTE - the state may be null in the case of the overriding evicting window operator\n247:         if (windowStateDescriptor != null) {\n248:             windowState =\n249:                     (InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>)\n250:                             getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, windowStateDescriptor);\n251:         }\n252: \n253:         // create the typed and helper states for merging windows\n254:         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n255: \n256:             // store a typed reference for the state of merging windows - sanity check\n257:             if (windowState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n258:                 windowMergingState = (InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>) windowState;\n259:             } else if (windowState != null) {\n260:                 throw new IllegalStateException(\n261:                         \"The window uses a merging assigner, but the window state is not mergeable.\");\n262:             }\n263: \n264:             @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n265:             final Class<Tuple2<W, W>> typedTuple = (Class<Tuple2<W, W>>) (Class<?>) Tuple2.class;\n266: \n267:             final TupleSerializer<Tuple2<W, W>> tupleSerializer =\n268:                     new TupleSerializer<>(\n269:                             typedTuple, new TypeSerializer[] {windowSerializer, windowSerializer});\n270: \n271:             final ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsStateDescriptor =\n272:                     new ListStateDescriptor<>(\"merging-window-set\", tupleSerializer);\n273: \n274:             // get the state that stores the merging sets\n275:             mergingSetsState =\n276:                     (InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>>)\n277:                             getOrCreateKeyedState(\n278:                                     VoidNamespaceSerializer.INSTANCE, mergingSetsStateDescriptor);\n279:             mergingSetsState.setCurrentNamespace(VoidNamespace.INSTANCE);\n280:         }\n281:     }\n282: \n283:     @Override\n284:     public void close() throws Exception {\n285:         super.close();\n286:         timestampedCollector = null;\n287:         triggerContext = null;\n288:         processContext = null;\n289:         windowAssignerContext = null;\n290:     }\n291: \n292:     @Override\n293:     public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n294:         final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n295:                 windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n296:                         element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n297: \n298:         // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n299:         boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n300: \n301:         final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n302: \n303:         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n304:             MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n305: \n306:             for (W window : elementWindows) {\n307: \n308:                 // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n309:                 // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n310:                 // actualWindow == window\n311:                 W actualWindow =\n312:                         mergingWindows.addWindow(\n313:                                 window,\n314:                                 new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n315:                                     @Override\n316:                                     public void merge(\n317:                                             W mergeResult,\n318:                                             Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n319:                                             W stateWindowResult,\n320:                                             Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n321:                                             throws Exception {\n322: \n323:                                         if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n324:                                                 && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n325:                                                         <= internalTimerService\n326:                                                                 .currentWatermark())) {\n327:                                             throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n328:                                                     \"The end timestamp of an \"\n329:                                                             + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n330:                                                             + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n331:                                                             + internalTimerService\n332:                                                                     .currentWatermark()\n333:                                                             + \" window: \"\n334:                                                             + mergeResult);\n335:                                         } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n336:                                             long currentProcessingTime =\n337:                                                     internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n338:                                             if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n339:                                                     <= currentProcessingTime) {\n340:                                                 throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n341:                                                         \"The end timestamp of a \"\n342:                                                                 + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n343:                                                                 + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n344:                                                                 + currentProcessingTime\n345:                                                                 + \" window: \"\n346:                                                                 + mergeResult);\n347:                                             }\n348:                                         }\n349: \n350:                                         triggerContext.key = key;\n351:                                         triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n352: \n353:                                         triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n354: \n355:                                         for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n356:                                             triggerContext.window = m;\n357:                                             triggerContext.clear();\n358:                                             deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n359:                                         }\n360: \n361:                                         // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n362:                                         // state window\n363:                                         windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\n364:                                                 stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n365:                                     }\n366:                                 });\n367: \n368:                 // drop if the window is already late\n369:                 if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n370:                     mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n371:                     continue;\n372:                 }\n373:                 isSkippedElement = false;\n374: \n375:                 W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n376:                 if (stateWindow == null) {\n377:                     throw new IllegalStateException(\n378:                             \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n379:                 }\n380: \n381:                 windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n382:                 windowState.add(element.getValue());\n383: \n384:                 triggerContext.key = key;\n385:                 triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n386: \n387:                 TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n388: \n389:                 if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n390:                     ACC contents = windowState.get();\n391:                     if (contents != null) {\n392:                         emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n393:                     }\n394:                 }\n395: \n396:                 if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n397:                     windowState.clear();\n398:                 }\n399:                 registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n400:             }\n401: \n402:             // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n403:             mergingWindows.persist();\n404:         } else {\n405:             for (W window : elementWindows) {\n406: \n407:                 // drop if the window is already late\n408:                 if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n409:                     continue;\n410:                 }\n411:                 isSkippedElement = false;\n412: \n413:                 windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n414:                 windowState.add(element.getValue());\n415: \n416:                 triggerContext.key = key;\n417:                 triggerContext.window = window;\n418: \n419:                 TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n420: \n421:                 if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n422:                     ACC contents = windowState.get();\n423:                     if (contents != null) {\n424:                         emitWindowContents(window, contents);\n425:                     }\n426:                 }\n427: \n428:                 if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n429:                     windowState.clear();\n430:                 }\n431:                 registerCleanupTimer(window);\n432:             }\n433:         }\n434: \n435:         // side output input event if\n436:         // element not handled by any window\n437:         // late arriving tag has been set\n438:         // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n439:         // element timestamp\n440:         if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n441:             if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n442:                 sideOutput(element);\n443:             } else {\n444:                 this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n445:             }\n446:         }\n447:     }\n448: \n449:     @Override\n450:     public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n451:         triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n452:         triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n453: \n454:         MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n455: \n456:         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n457:             mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n458:             W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n459:             if (stateWindow == null) {\n460:                 // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n461:                 // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n462:                 // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n463:                 return;\n464:             } else {\n465:                 windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n466:             }\n467:         } else {\n468:             windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n469:             mergingWindows = null;\n470:         }\n471: \n472:         TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n473: \n474:         if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n475:             ACC contents = windowState.get();\n476:             if (contents != null) {\n477:                 emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n478:             }\n479:         }\n480: \n481:         if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n482:             windowState.clear();\n483:         }\n484: \n485:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n486:                 && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n487:             clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n488:         }\n489: \n490:         if (mergingWindows != null) {\n491:             // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n492:             mergingWindows.persist();\n493:         }\n494:     }\n495: \n496:     @Override\n497:     public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n498:         triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n499:         triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n500: \n501:         MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n502: \n503:         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n504:             mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n505:             W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n506:             if (stateWindow == null) {\n507:                 // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n508:                 // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n509:                 // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n510:                 return;\n511:             } else {\n512:                 windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n513:             }\n514:         } else {\n515:             windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n516:             mergingWindows = null;\n517:         }\n518: \n519:         TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n520: \n521:         if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n522:             ACC contents = windowState.get();\n523:             if (contents != null) {\n524:                 emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n525:             }\n526:         }\n527: \n528:         if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n529:             windowState.clear();\n530:         }\n531: \n532:         if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n533:                 && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n534:             clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n535:         }\n536: \n537:         if (mergingWindows != null) {\n538:             // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n539:             mergingWindows.persist();\n540:         }\n541:     }\n542: \n543:     @Override\n544:     public OperatorAttributes getOperatorAttributes() {\n545:         boolean isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream =\n546:                 windowAssigner instanceof GlobalWindows\n547:                         && trigger instanceof GlobalWindows.EndOfStreamTrigger;\n548:         return new OperatorAttributesBuilder()\n549:                 .setOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream(isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream)\n550:                 .build();\n551:     }\n552: \n553:     /**\n554:      * Drops all state for the given window and calls {@link Trigger#clear(Window,\n555:      * Trigger.TriggerContext)}.\n556:      *\n557:      * <p>The caller must ensure that the correct key is set in the state backend and the\n558:      * triggerContext object.\n559:      */\n560:     private void clearAllState(\n561:             W window, AppendingState<IN, ACC> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n562:             throws Exception {\n563:         windowState.clear();\n564:         triggerContext.clear();\n565:         processContext.window = window;\n566:         processContext.clear();\n567:         if (mergingWindows != null) {\n568:             mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n569:             mergingWindows.persist();\n570:         }\n571:     }\n572: \n573:     /** Emits the contents of the given window using the {@link InternalWindowFunction}. */\n574:     @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n575:     private void emitWindowContents(W window, ACC contents) throws Exception {\n576:         timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n577:         processContext.window = window;\n578:         userFunction.process(\n579:                 triggerContext.key, window, processContext, contents, timestampedCollector);\n580:     }\n581: \n582:     /**\n583:      * Write skipped late arriving element to SideOutput.\n584:      *\n585:      * @param element skipped late arriving element to side output\n586:      */\n587:     protected void sideOutput(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n588:         output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element);\n589:     }\n590: \n591:     /**\n592:      * Retrieves the {@link MergingWindowSet} for the currently active key. The caller must ensure\n593:      * that the correct key is set in the state backend.\n594:      *\n595:      * <p>The caller must also ensure to properly persist changes to state using {@link\n596:      * MergingWindowSet#persist()}.\n597:      */\n598:     protected MergingWindowSet<W> getMergingWindowSet() throws Exception {\n599:         @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n600:         MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W> mergingAssigner =\n601:                 (MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W>) windowAssigner;\n602:         return new MergingWindowSet<>(mergingAssigner, mergingSetsState);\n603:     }\n604: \n605:     /**\n606:      * Returns {@code true} if the watermark is after the end timestamp plus the allowed lateness of\n607:      * the given window.\n608:      */\n609:     protected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\n610:         return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n611:                 && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\n612:     }\n613: \n614:     /**\n615:      * Decide if a record is currently late, based on current watermark and allowed lateness.\n616:      *\n617:      * @param element The element to check\n618:      * @return The element for which should be considered when sideoutputs\n619:      */\n620:     protected boolean isElementLate(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n621:         return (windowAssigner.isEventTime())\n622:                 && (element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n623:                         <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark());\n624:     }\n625: \n626:     /**\n627:      * Registers a timer to cleanup the content of the window.\n628:      *\n629:      * @param window the window whose state to discard\n630:      */\n631:     protected void registerCleanupTimer(W window) {\n632:         long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n633:         if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n634:             // don't set a GC timer for \"end of time\"\n635:             return;\n636:         }\n637: \n638:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n639:             triggerContext.registerEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n640:         } else {\n641:             triggerContext.registerProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n642:         }\n643:     }\n644: \n645:     /**\n646:      * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\n647:      *\n648:      * @param window the window whose state to discard\n649:      */\n650:     protected void deleteCleanupTimer(W window) {\n651:         long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n652:         if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n653:             // no need to clean up because we didn't set one\n654:             return;\n655:         }\n656:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n657:             triggerContext.deleteEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n658:         } else {\n659:             triggerContext.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n660:         }\n661:     }\n662: \n663:     /**\n664:      * Returns the cleanup time for a window, which is {@code window.maxTimestamp +\n665:      * allowedLateness}. In case this leads to a value greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} then a\n666:      * cleanup time of {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} is returned.\n667:      *\n668:      * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\n669:      */\n670:     private long cleanupTime(W window) {\n671:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n672:             long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\n673:             return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\n674:         } else {\n675:             return window.maxTimestamp();\n676:         }\n677:     }\n678: \n679:     /** Returns {@code true} if the given time is the cleanup time for the given window. */\n680:     protected final boolean isCleanupTime(W window, long time) {\n681:         return time == cleanupTime(window);\n682:     }\n683: \n684:     /**\n685:      * Base class for per-window {@link KeyedStateStore KeyedStateStores}. Used to allow per-window\n686:      * state access for {@link\n687:      * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction}.\n688:      */\n689:     public abstract class AbstractPerWindowStateStore extends DefaultKeyedStateStore {\n690: \n691:         // we have this in the base class even though it's not used in MergingKeyStore so that\n692:         // we can always set it and ignore what actual implementation we have\n693:         protected W window;\n694: \n695:         public AbstractPerWindowStateStore(\n696:                 KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n697:             super(\n698:                     keyedStateBackend,\n699:                     new SerializerFactory() {\n700:                         @Override\n701:                         public <T> TypeSerializer<T> createSerializer(\n702:                                 TypeInformation<T> typeInformation) {\n703:                             return typeInformation.createSerializer(\n704:                                     executionConfig.getSerializerConfig());\n705:                         }\n706:                     });\n707:         }\n708:     }\n709: \n710:     /**\n711:      * Special {@link AbstractPerWindowStateStore} that doesn't allow access to per-window state.\n712:      */\n713:     public class MergingWindowStateStore extends AbstractPerWindowStateStore {\n714: \n715:         public MergingWindowStateStore(\n716:                 KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n717:             super(keyedStateBackend, executionConfig);\n718:         }\n719: \n720:         @Override\n721:         public <T> ValueState<T> getState(ValueStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n722:             throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n723:                     \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n724:         }\n725: \n726:         @Override\n727:         public <T> ListState<T> getListState(ListStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n728:             throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n729:                     \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n730:         }\n731: \n732:         @Override\n733:         public <T> ReducingState<T> getReducingState(ReducingStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n734:             throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n735:                     \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n736:         }\n737: \n738:         @Override\n739:         public <IN, ACC, OUT> AggregatingState<IN, OUT> getAggregatingState(\n740:                 AggregatingStateDescriptor<IN, ACC, OUT> stateProperties) {\n741:             throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n742:                     \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n743:         }\n744: \n745:         @Override\n746:         public <UK, UV> MapState<UK, UV> getMapState(MapStateDescriptor<UK, UV> stateProperties) {\n747:             throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n748:                     \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n749:         }\n750:     }\n751: \n752:     /**\n753:      * Regular per-window state store for use with {@link\n754:      * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction}.\n755:      */\n756:     public class PerWindowStateStore extends AbstractPerWindowStateStore {\n757: \n758:         public PerWindowStateStore(\n759:                 KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n760:             super(keyedStateBackend, executionConfig);\n761:         }\n762: \n763:         @Override\n764:         protected <S extends State> S getPartitionedState(StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor)\n765:                 throws Exception {\n766:             return keyedStateBackend.getPartitionedState(window, windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n767:         }\n768:     }\n769: \n770:     /**\n771:      * A utility class for handling {@code ProcessWindowFunction} invocations. This can be reused by\n772:      * setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept in the\n773:      * {@code WindowContext}.\n774:      */\n775:     public class WindowContext implements InternalWindowFunction.InternalWindowContext {\n776:         protected W window;\n777: \n778:         protected AbstractPerWindowStateStore windowState;\n779: \n780:         public WindowContext(W window) {\n781:             this.window = window;\n782:             this.windowState =\n783:                     windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner\n784:                             ? new MergingWindowStateStore(\n785:                                     getKeyedStateBackend(), getExecutionConfig())\n786:                             : new PerWindowStateStore(getKeyedStateBackend(), getExecutionConfig());\n787:         }\n788: \n789:         @Override\n790:         public String toString() {\n791:             return \"WindowContext{Window = \" + window.toString() + \"}\";\n792:         }\n793: \n794:         public void clear() throws Exception {\n795:             userFunction.clear(window, this);\n796:         }\n797: \n798:         @Override\n799:         public long currentProcessingTime() {\n800:             return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n801:         }\n802: \n803:         @Override\n804:         public long currentWatermark() {\n805:             return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n806:         }\n807: \n808:         @Override\n809:         public KeyedStateStore windowState() {\n810:             this.windowState.window = this.window;\n811:             return this.windowState;\n812:         }\n813: \n814:         @Override\n815:         public KeyedStateStore globalState() {\n816:             return WindowOperator.this.getKeyedStateStore();\n817:         }\n818: \n819:         @Override\n820:         public <X> void output(OutputTag<X> outputTag, X value) {\n821:             if (outputTag == null) {\n822:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"OutputTag must not be null.\");\n823:             }\n824:             output.collect(outputTag, new StreamRecord<>(value, window.maxTimestamp()));\n825:         }\n826:     }\n827: \n828:     /**\n829:      * {@code Context} is a utility for handling {@code Trigger} invocations. It can be reused by\n830:      * setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept in the\n831:      * {@code Context}\n832:      */\n833:     public class Context implements Trigger.OnMergeContext {\n834:         protected K key;\n835:         protected W window;\n836: \n837:         protected Collection<W> mergedWindows;\n838: \n839:         public Context(K key, W window) {\n840:             this.key = key;\n841:             this.window = window;\n842:         }\n843: \n844:         @Override\n845:         public MetricGroup getMetricGroup() {\n846:             return WindowOperator.this.getMetricGroup();\n847:         }\n848: \n849:         public long getCurrentWatermark() {\n850:             return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n851:         }\n852: \n853:         @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n854:         public <S extends State> S getPartitionedState(StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor) {\n855:             try {\n856:                 return WindowOperator.this.getPartitionedState(\n857:                         window, windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n858:             } catch (Exception e) {\n859:                 throw new RuntimeException(\"Could not retrieve state\", e);\n860:             }\n861:         }\n862: \n863:         @Override\n864:         public <S extends MergingState<?, ?>> void mergePartitionedState(\n865:                 StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor) {\n866:             if (mergedWindows != null && mergedWindows.size() > 0) {\n867:                 try {\n868:                     S rawState =\n869:                             getKeyedStateBackend()\n870:                                     .getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n871: \n872:                     if (rawState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n873:                         @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n874:                         InternalMergingState<K, W, ?, ?, ?> mergingState =\n875:                                 (InternalMergingState<K, W, ?, ?, ?>) rawState;\n876:                         mergingState.mergeNamespaces(window, mergedWindows);\n877:                     } else {\n878:                         throw new IllegalArgumentException(\n879:                                 \"The given state descriptor does not refer to a mergeable state (MergingState)\");\n880:                     }\n881:                 } catch (Exception e) {\n882:                     throw new RuntimeException(\"Error while merging state.\", e);\n883:                 }\n884:             }\n885:         }\n886: \n887:         @Override\n888:         public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n889:             return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n890:         }\n891: \n892:         @Override\n893:         public void registerProcessingTimeTimer(long time) {\n894:             internalTimerService.registerProcessingTimeTimer(window, time);\n895:         }\n896: \n897:         @Override\n898:         public void registerEventTimeTimer(long time) {\n899:             internalTimerService.registerEventTimeTimer(window, time);\n900:         }\n901: \n902:         @Override\n903:         public void deleteProcessingTimeTimer(long time) {\n904:             internalTimerService.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(window, time);\n905:         }\n906: \n907:         @Override\n908:         public void deleteEventTimeTimer(long time) {\n909:             internalTimerService.deleteEventTimeTimer(window, time);\n910:         }\n911: \n912:         public TriggerResult onElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n913:             return trigger.onElement(element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), window, this);\n914:         }\n915: \n916:         public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time) throws Exception {\n917:             return trigger.onProcessingTime(time, window, this);\n918:         }\n919: \n920:         public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time) throws Exception {\n921:             return trigger.onEventTime(time, window, this);\n922:         }\n923: \n924:         public void onMerge(Collection<W> mergedWindows) throws Exception {\n925:             this.mergedWindows = mergedWindows;\n926:             trigger.onMerge(window, this);\n927:         }\n928: \n929:         public void clear() throws Exception {\n930:             trigger.clear(window, this);\n931:         }\n932: \n933:         @Override\n934:         public String toString() {\n935:             return \"Context{\" + \"key=\" + key + \", window=\" + window + '}';\n936:         }\n937:     }\n938: \n939:     /** Internal class for keeping track of in-flight timers. */\n940:     protected static class Timer<K, W extends Window> implements Comparable<Timer<K, W>> {\n941:         protected long timestamp;\n942:         protected K key;\n943:         protected W window;\n944: \n945:         public Timer(long timestamp, K key, W window) {\n946:             this.timestamp = timestamp;\n947:             this.key = key;\n948:             this.window = window;\n949:         }\n950: \n951:         @Override\n952:         public int compareTo(Timer<K, W> o) {\n953:             return Long.compare(this.timestamp, o.timestamp);\n954:         }\n955: \n956:         @Override\n957:         public boolean equals(Object o) {\n958:             if (this == o) {\n959:                 return true;\n960:             }\n961:             if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass()) {\n962:                 return false;\n963:             }\n964: \n965:             Timer<?, ?> timer = (Timer<?, ?>) o;\n966: \n967:             return timestamp == timer.timestamp\n968:                     && key.equals(timer.key)\n969:                     && window.equals(timer.window);\n970:         }\n971: \n972:         @Override\n973:         public int hashCode() {\n974:             int result = (int) (timestamp ^ (timestamp >>> 32));\n975:             result = 31 * result + key.hashCode();\n976:             result = 31 * result + window.hashCode();\n977:             return result;\n978:         }\n979: \n980:         @Override\n981:         public String toString() {\n982:             return \"Timer{\"\n983:                     + \"timestamp=\"\n984:                     + timestamp\n985:                     + \", key=\"\n986:                     + key\n987:                     + \", window=\"\n988:                     + window\n989:                     + '}';\n990:         }\n991:     }\n992: \n993:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n994:     // Getters for testing\n995:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n996: \n997:     @VisibleForTesting\n998:     public Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> getTrigger() {\n999:         return trigger;\n1000:     }\n1001: \n1002:     @VisibleForTesting\n1003:     public KeySelector<IN, K> getKeySelector() {\n1004:         return keySelector;\n1005:     }\n1006: \n1007:     @VisibleForTesting\n1008:     public WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> getWindowAssigner() {\n1009:         return windowAssigner;\n1010:     }\n1011: \n1012:     @VisibleForTesting\n1013:     public StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> getStateDescriptor() {\n1014:         return windowStateDescriptor;\n1015:     }\n1016: }\n1017: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc@de39c3e1777c43fe3363b3fd7784bf46b57a99c2/-/blob/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java"

not be dropped because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\\n2620:         // after the current watermark\\n2621:         testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\\n2626: \\n2627:         expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\\n2628: \\n2629:         // this will merge the two sessions into one\\n2630:   "content": "{\"content\":\"2650:         expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\\\"key2-1\\\", 14500L, 17500L), 17499));\\n2651:         expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\\n2652: \\n2653:         testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\\n2654:         expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\\n2655: \\n2656:         ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\\n2657:         ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\\n2658:                 testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\\n2659:         TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\\n2660:                 \\\"Output was not correct.\\\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\\n2661:         TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\\n2662:                 \\\"SideOutput was not correct.\\\",\\n2663:                 sideExpected,\\n2664:                 (Iterable) sideActual,\\n2665:                 new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\\n2666:         testHarness.close();\\n2667:     }\\n2668: \\n2669:     @Test\\n2670:     void testDropDueToLatenessSessionWithLatenessPurgingTrigger() throws Exception {\\n2671: \\n2672:         // this has the same output as testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionZeroLateness() because\\n2673:         // the allowed lateness is too small to make a difference\\n2674: \\n2675:         final int gapSize = 3;\\n2676:         final long lateness = 10;\\n2677: \\n2678:         ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\\n2679:                 new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\\n2680:                         \\\"window-contents\\\",\\n2681: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc@de39c3e1777c43fe3363b3fd7784bf46b57a99c2/-/blob/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java%3FL2650-2680\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2650:         expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 14500L, 17500L), 17499));\n2651:         expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n2652: \n2653:         testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n2654:         expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n2655: \n2656:         ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n2657:         ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n2658:                 testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n2659:         TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n2660:                 \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n2661:         TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n2662:                 \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n2663:                 sideExpected,\n2664:                 (Iterable) sideActual,\n2665:                 new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n2666:         testHarness.close();\n2667:     }\n2668: \n2669:     @Test\n2670:     void testDropDueToLatenessSessionWithLatenessPurgingTrigger() throws Exception {\n2671: \n2672:         // this has the same output as testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionZeroLateness() because\n2673:         // the allowed lateness is too small to make a difference\n2674: \n2675:         final int gapSize = 3;\n2676:         final long lateness = 10;\n2677: \n2678:         ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n2679:                 new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n2680:                         \"window-contents\",\n2681: ",

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one\\n3:  * or more contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file\\n4:  * distributed with this work for additional information\\n5:  * regarding copyright ownership.  The ASF licenses this file\\n6:  * to you under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the\\n7:  * \\\"License\\\"); you may not use this file except in compliance\\n8:  * with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n9:  *\\n10:  *     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n11:  *\\n12:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n13:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n14:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n15:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n16:  * limitations under the License.\\n17:  */\\n18: \\n19: package org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing;\\n20: \\n21: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ListState;\\n22: import org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple.Tuple2;\\n23: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.MergingWindowAssigner;\\n24: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.Window;\\n25: \\n26: import org.slf4j.Logger;\\n27: import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\\n28: \\n29: import java.util.ArrayList;\\n30: import java.util.Collection;\\n31: import java.util.HashMap;\\n32: import java.util.List;\\n33: import java.util.Map;\\n34: import java.util.stream.Collectors;\\n35: \\n36: /**\\n37:  * Utility for keeping track of merging {@link Window Windows} when using a {@link\\n38:  * MergingWindowAssigner} in a {@link WindowOperator}.\\n39:  *\\n40:  * <p>When merging windows, we keep one of the original windows as the state window, i.e. the window\\n41:  * that is used as namespace to store the window elements. Elements from the state windows of merged\\n42:  * windows must be merged into this one state window. We keep a mapping from in-flight window to\\n43:  * state window that can be queried using {@link #getStateWindow(Window)}.\\n44:  *\\n45:  * <p>A new window can be added to the set of in-flight windows using {@link #addWindow(Window,\\n46:  * MergeFunction)}. This might merge other windows and the caller must react accordingly in the\\n47:  * {@link MergeFunction#merge(Object, Collection, Object, Collection)} and adjust the outside view\\n48:  * of windows and state.\\n49:  *\\n50:  * <p>Windows can be removed from the set of windows using {@link #retireWindow(Window)}.\\n51:  *\\n52:  * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that this set is keeping track of.\\n53:  */\\n54: public class MergingWindowSet<W extends Window> {\\n55: \\n56:     private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MergingWindowSet.class);\\n57: \\n58:     /**\\n59:      * Mapping from window to the window that keeps the window state. When we are incrementally\\n60:      * merging windows starting from some window we keep that starting window as the state window to\\n61:      * prevent costly state juggling.\\n62:      */\\n63:     private final Map<W, W> mapping;\\n64: \\n65:     /**\\n66:      * Mapping when we created the {@code MergingWindowSet}. We use this to decide whether we need\\n67:      * to persist any changes to state.\\n68:      */\\n69:     private final Map<W, W> initialMapping;\\n70: \\n71:     private final ListState<Tuple2<W, W>> state;\\n72: \\n73:     /** Our window assigner. */\\n74:     private final MergingWindowAssigner<?, W> windowAssigner;\\n75: \\n76:     /** Restores a {@link MergingWindowSet} from the given state. */\\n77:     public MergingWindowSet(\\n78:             MergingWindowAssigner<?, W> windowAssigner, ListState<Tuple2<W, W>> state)\\n79:             throws Exception {\\n80:         this.windowAssigner = windowAssigner;\\n81:         mapping = new HashMap<>();\\n82: \\n83:         Iterable<Tuple2<W, W>> windowState = state.get();\\n84:         if (windowState != null) {\\n85:             for (Tuple2<W, W> window : windowState) {\\n86:                 mapping.put(window.f0, window.f1);\\n87:             }\\n88:         }\\n89: \\n90:         this.state = state;\\n91: \\n92:         initialMapping = new HashMap<>();\\n93:         initialMapping.putAll(mapping);\\n94:     }\\n95: \\n96:     /**\\n97:      * Persist the updated mapping to the given state if the mapping changed since initialization.\\n98:      */\\n99:     public void persist() throws Exception {\\n100:         if (!mapping.equals(initialMapping)) {\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc@de39c3e1777c43fe3363b3fd7784bf46b57a99c2/-/blob/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/MergingWindowSet.java%3FL1-100\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one\n3:  * or more contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file\n4:  * distributed with this work for additional information\n5:  * regarding copyright ownership.  The ASF licenses this file\n6:  * to you under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the\n7:  * \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance\n8:  * with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n9:  *\n10:  *     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n11:  *\n12:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n13:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n14:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n15:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n16:  * limitations under the License.\n17:  */\n18: \n19: package org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing;\n20: \n21: import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ListState;\n22: import org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple.Tuple2;\n23: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.MergingWindowAssigner;\n24: import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.Window;\n25: \n26: import org.slf4j.Logger;\n27: import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n28: \n29: import java.util.ArrayList;\n30: import java.util.Collection;\n31: import java.util.HashMap;\n32: import java.util.List;\n33: import java.util.Map;\n34: import java.util.stream.Collectors;\n35: \n36: /**\n37:  * Utility for keeping track of merging {@link Window Windows} when using a {@link\n38:  * MergingWindowAssigner} in a {@link WindowOperator}.\n39:  *\n40:  * <p>When merging windows, we keep one of the original windows as the state window, i.e. the window\n41:  * that is used as namespace to store the window elements. Elements from the state windows of merged\n42:  * windows must be merged into this one state window. We keep a mapping from in-flight window to\n43:  * state window that can be queried using {@link #getStateWindow(Window)}.\n44:  *\n45:  * <p>A new window can be added to the set of in-flight windows using {@link #addWindow(Window,\n46:  * MergeFunction)}. This might merge other windows and the caller must react accordingly in the\n47:  * {@link MergeFunction#merge(Object, Collection, Object, Collection)} and adjust the outside view\n48:  * of windows and state.\n49:  *\n50:  * <p>Windows can be removed from the set of windows using {@link #retireWindow(Window)}.\n51:  *\n52:  * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that this set is keeping track of.\n53:  */\n54: public class MergingWindowSet<W extends Window> {\n55: \n56:     private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MergingWindowSet.class);\n57: \n58:     /**\n59:      * Mapping from window to the window that keeps the window state. When we are incrementally\n60:      * merging windows starting from some window we keep that starting window as the state window to\n61:      * prevent costly state juggling.\n62:      */\n63:     private final Map<W, W> mapping;\n64: \n65:     /**\n66:      * Mapping when we created the {@code MergingWindowSet}. We use this to decide whether we need\n67:      * to persist any changes to state.\n68:      */\n69:     private final Map<W, W> initialMapping;\n70: \n71:     private final ListState<Tuple2<W, W>> state;\n72: \n73:     /** Our window assigner. */\n74:     private final MergingWindowAssigner<?, W> windowAssigner;\n75: \n76:     /** Restores a {@link MergingWindowSet} from the given state. */\n77:     public MergingWindowSet(\n78:             MergingWindowAssigner<?, W> windowAssigner, ListState<Tuple2<W, W>> state)\n79:             throws Exception {\n80:         this.windowAssigner = windowAssigner;\n81:         mapping = new HashMap<>();\n82: \n83:         Iterable<Tuple2<W, W>> windowState = state.get();\n84:         if (windowState != null) {\n85:             for (Tuple2<W, W> window : windowState) {\n86:                 mapping.put(window.f0, window.f1);\n87:             }\n88:         }\n89: \n90:         this.state = state;\n91: \n92:         initialMapping = new HashMap<>();\n93:         initialMapping.putAll(mapping);\n94:     }\n95: \n96:     /**\n97:      * Persist the updated mapping to the given state if the mapping changed since initialization.\n98:      */\n99:     public void persist() throws Exception {\n100:         if (!mapping.equals(initialMapping)) {\n101: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc@de39c3e1777c43fe3363b3fd7784bf46b57a99c2/-/blob/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/MergingWindowSet.java%3FL1-100"
  }
}
13. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 200,
  "path": "flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/MergingWindowSet.java",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc",
  "startLine": 100
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"100:         if (!mapping.equals(initialMapping)) {\\n101:             state.update(\\n102:                     mapping.entrySet().stream()\\n103:                             .map((w) -> new Tuple2<>(w.getKey(), w.getValue()))\\n104:                             .collect(Collectors.toList()));\\n105:         }\\n106:     }\\n107: \\n108:     /**\\n109:      * Returns the state window for the given in-flight {@code Window}. The state window is the\\n110:      * {@code Window} in which we keep the actual state of a given in-flight window. Windows might\\n111:      * expand but we keep to original state window for keeping the elements of the window to avoid\\n112:      * costly state juggling.\\n113:      *\\n114:      * @param window The window for which to get the state window.\\n115:      */\\n116:     public W getStateWindow(W window) {\\n117:         return mapping.get(window);\\n118:     }\\n119: \\n120:     /**\\n121:      * Removes the given window from the set of in-flight windows.\\n122:      *\\n123:      * @param window The {@code Window} to remove.\\n124:      */\\n125:     public void retireWindow(W window) {\\n126:         W removed = this.mapping.remove(window);\\n127:         if (removed == null) {\\n128:             throw new IllegalStateException(\\n129:                     \\\"Window \\\" + window + \\\" is not in in-flight window set.\\\");\\n130:         }\\n131:     }\\n132: \\n133:     /**\\n134:      * Adds a new {@code Window} to the set of in-flight windows. It might happen that this triggers\\n135:      * merging of previously in-flight windows. In that case, the provided {@link MergeFunction} is\\n136:      * called.\\n137:      *\\n138:      * <p>This returns the window that is the representative of the added window after adding. This\\n139:      * can either be the new window itself, if no merge occurred, or the newly merged window. Adding\\n140:      * an element to a window or calling trigger functions should only happen on the returned\\n141:      * representative. This way, we never have to deal with a new window that is immediately\\n142:      * swallowed up by another window.\\n143:      *\\n144:      * <p>If the new window is merged, the {@code MergeFunction} callback arguments also don't\\n145:      * contain the new window as part of the list of merged windows.\\n146:      *\\n147:      * @param newWindow The new {@code Window} to add.\\n148:      * @param mergeFunction The callback to be invoked in case a merge occurs.\\n149:      * @return The {@code Window} that new new {@code Window} ended up in. This can also be the new\\n150:      *     {@code Window} itself in case no merge occurred.\\n151:      * @throws Exception\\n152:      */\\n153:     public W addWindow(W newWindow, MergeFunction<W> mergeFunction) throws Exception {\\n154: \\n155:         List<W> windows = new ArrayList<>();\\n156: \\n157:         windows.addAll(this.mapping.keySet());\\n158:         windows.add(newWindow);\\n159: \\n160:         final Map<W, Collection<W>> mergeResults = new HashMap<>();\\n161:         windowAssigner.mergeWindows(\\n162:                 windows,\\n163: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc@de39c3e1777c43fe3363b3fd7784bf46b57a99c2/-/blob/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/MergingWindowSet.java%3FL100-200\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "100:         if (!mapping.equals(initialMapping)) {\n101:             state.update(\n102:                     mapping.entrySet().stream()\n103:                             .map((w) -> new Tuple2<>(w.getKey(), w.getValue()))\n104:                             .collect(Collectors.toList()));\n105:         }\n106:     }\n107: \n108:     /**\n109:      * Returns the state window for the given in-flight {@code Window}. The state window is the\n110:      * {@code Window} in which we keep the actual state of a given in-flight window. Windows might\n111:      * expand but we keep to original state window for keeping the elements of the window to avoid\n112:      * costly state juggling.\n113:      *\n114:      * @param window The window for which to get the state window.\n115:      */\n116:     public W getStateWindow(W window) {\n117:         return mapping.get(window);\n118:     }\n119: \n120:     /**\n121:      * Removes the given window from the set of in-flight windows.\n122:      *\n123:      * @param window The {@code Window} to remove.\n124:      */\n125:     public void retireWindow(W window) {\n126:         W removed = this.mapping.remove(window);\n127:         if (removed == null) {\n128:             throw new IllegalStateException(\n129:                     \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n130:         }\n131:     }\n132: \n133:     /**\n134:      * Adds a new {@code Window} to the set of in-flight windows. It might happen that this triggers\n135:      * merging of previously in-flight windows. In that case, the provided {@link MergeFunction} is\n136:      * called.\n137:      *\n138:      * <p>This returns the window that is the representative of the added window after adding. This\n139:      * can either be the new window itself, if no merge occurred, or the newly merged window. Adding\n140:      * an element to a window or calling trigger functions should only happen on the returned\n141:      * representative. This way, we never have to deal with a new window that is immediately\n142:      * swallowed up by another window.\n143:      *\n144:      * <p>If the new window is merged, the {@code MergeFunction} callback arguments also don't\n145:      * contain the new window as part of the list of merged windows.\n146:      *\n147:      * @param newWindow The new {@code Window} to add.\n148:      * @param mergeFunction The callback to be invoked in case a merge occurs.\n149:      * @return The {@code Window} that new new {@code Window} ended up in. This can also be the new\n150:      *     {@code Window} itself in case no merge occurred.\n151:      * @throws Exception\n152:      */\n153:     public W addWindow(W newWindow, MergeFunction<W> mergeFunction) throws Exception {\n154: \n155:         List<W> windows = new ArrayList<>();\n156: \n157:         windows.addAll(this.mapping.keySet());\n158:         windows.add(newWindow);\n159: \n160:         final Map<W, Collection<W>> mergeResults = new HashMap<>();\n161:         windowAssigner.mergeWindows(\n162:                 windows,\n163:                 new MergingWindowAssigner.MergeCallback<W>() {\n164:                     @Override\n165:                     public void merge(Collection<W> toBeMerged, W mergeResult) {\n166:                         if (LOG.isDebugEnabled()) {\n167:                             LOG.debug(\"Merging {} into {}\", toBeMerged, mergeResult);\n168:                         }\n169:                         mergeResults.put(mergeResult, toBeMerged);\n170:                     }\n171:                 });\n172: \n173:         W resultWindow = newWindow;\n174:         boolean mergedNewWindow = false;\n175: \n176:         // perform the merge\n177:         for (Map.Entry<W, Collection<W>> c : mergeResults.entrySet()) {\n178:             W mergeResult = c.getKey();\n179:             Collection<W> mergedWindows = c.getValue();\n180: \n181:             // if our new window is in the merged windows make the merge result the\n182:             // result window\n183:             if (mergedWindows.remove(newWindow)) {\n184:                 mergedNewWindow = true;\n185:                 resultWindow = mergeResult;\n186:             }\n187: \n188:             // pick any of the merged windows and choose that window's state window\n189:             // as the state window for the merge result\n190:             W mergedStateWindow = this.mapping.get(mergedWindows.iterator().next());\n191: \n192:             // figure out the state windows that we are merging\n193:             List<W> mergedStateWindows = new ArrayList<>();\n194:             for (W mergedWindow : mergedWindows) {\n195:                 W res = this.mapping.remove(mergedWindow);\n196:                 if (res != null) {\n197:                     mergedStateWindows.add(res);\n198:                 }\n199:             }\n200: \n201: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc@de39c3e1777c43fe3363b3fd7784bf46b57a99c2/-/blob/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/MergingWindowSet.java%3FL100-200"
  }
}

Output

  "content": "{\"content\":\"200: \\n201:             this.mapping.put(mergeResult, mergedStateWindow);\\n202: \\n203:             // don't put the target state window into the merged windows\\n204:             mergedStateWindows.remove(mergedStateWindow);\\n205: \\n206:             // don't merge the new window itself, it never had any state associated with it\\n207:             // i.e. if we are only merging one pre-existing window into itself\\n208:             // without extending the pre-existing window\\n209:             if (!(mergedWindows.contains(mergeResult) && mergedWindows.size() == 1)) {\\n210:                 mergeFunction.merge(\\n211:                         mergeResult,\\n212:                         mergedWindows,\\n213:                         this.mapping.get(mergeResult),\\n214:                         mergedStateWindows);\\n215:             }\\n216:         }\\n217: \\n218:         // the new window created a new, self-contained window without merging\\n219:         if (mergeResults.isEmpty() || (resultWindow.equals(newWindow) && !mergedNewWindow)) {\\n220:             this.mapping.put(resultWindow, resultWindow);\\n221:         }\\n222: \\n223:         return resultWindow;\\n224:     }\\n225: \\n226:     /**\\n227:      * Callback for {@link #addWindow(Window, MergeFunction)}.\\n228:      *\\n229:      * @param <W>\\n230:      */\\n231: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc@de39c3e1777c43fe3363b3fd7784bf46b57a99c2/-/blob/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/MergingWindowSet.java%3FL200-230\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "200: \n201:             this.mapping.put(mergeResult, mergedStateWindow);\n202: \n203:             // don't put the target state window into the merged windows\n204:             mergedStateWindows.remove(mergedStateWindow);\n205: \n206:             // don't merge the new window itself, it never had any state associated with it\n207:             // i.e. if we are only merging one pre-existing window into itself\n208:             // without extending the pre-existing window\n209:             if (!(mergedWindows.contains(mergeResult) && mergedWindows.size() == 1)) {\n210:                 mergeFunction.merge(\n211:                         mergeResult,\n212:                         mergedWindows,\n213:                         this.mapping.get(mergeResult),\n214:                         mergedStateWindows);\n215:             }\n216:         }\n217: \n218:         // the new window created a new, self-contained window without merging\n219:         if (mergeResults.isEmpty() || (resultWindow.equals(newWindow) && !mergedNewWindow)) {\n220:             this.mapping.put(resultWindow, resultWindow);\n221:         }\n222: \n223:         return resultWindow;\n224:     }\n225: \n226:     /**\n227:      * Callback for {@link #addWindow(Window, MergeFunction)}.\n228:      *\n229:      * @param <W>\n230:      */\n231: ",

22. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/test_scenario.md << 'EOF'\n# Test Scenario: Late Data Side Output for Session Windows\n\n## Setup:\n- Session window gap: 3 seconds\n- Allowed lateness: 0\n- sideOutputLateData enabled\n\n## Events:\n1. Element E1 (key=key1, value=1) @ timestamp=1000\n   - Creates window [1000, 1003]\n   - Watermark = 1000\n\n2. Watermark = 2500\n\n3. Element E2 (key=key1, value=1) @ timestamp=2000\n   - Assigned to window [2000, 2003]\n   - But does this merge with [1000, 1003]?\n   - Gap is 3, so 2000 - 1003 = 997 > 3, no merge\n   - Creates new window [2000, 2003]\n   - Watermark = 2500\n\n4. Watermark = 2005\n\n5. Element E3 (key=key1, value=1) @ timestamp=2002\n   - isElementLate(E3) = (2002 + 0 <= 2005) = true\n   - Assigned to window [2002, 2005]\n   - Merges with [2000, 2003]? 2005 > 2000 && 2003 > 2002? Yes, merges\n   - Merged window = [2000, 2005]\n   - isWindowLate([2000, 2005]) = (2005 + 0 <= 2005) = true\n   - So window is dropped! But the element could have been merged into the existing [2000, 2003]\n\n## Expected Behavior:\nElement E3 should be:\n- Added to the merged window [2000, 2005], OR\n- If merged window is late, sideoutput to late data tag\n\n## Actual Behavior (Bug):\n- Merged window is late, so element is dropped\n- Element is marked as \"skipped\"\n- At the end, isSkippedElement=true AND isElementLate=true\n- So element is sideoutput as late (which is correct)\n\nWait, this seems correct... Let me think about another scenario...\n\n## Alternative Scenario:\nWhat if the issue is that we should check element lateness BEFORE assigning windows, and if it's late, we should still try to merge it into existing windows?\n\nActually, I think the real issue might be:\n- An element marked as \"late\" (isElementLate=true) should still be checked for merging\n- But currently, we don't check isElementLate before calling addWindow\n- So the element is added to the merging window set\n- But then the merged result is checked for lateness\n- If the merged result is late, we skip it\n- And the element is lost (not added to any window)\n\nThis could be the bug! The element should be added to the window if the window is not late, regardless of whether the element itself is late.\n\nLooking at the code again at line 369:\n```java\nif (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n    continue;\n}\nisSkippedElement = false;\n// ... add element\n```\n\nSo if the window is not late, we add the element. This should work correctly...\n\nUnless... wait, maybe the issue is that we're retiring the window when it's late, which removes it from the in-flight windows. Then later, when we check if the element should be sideoutput, we're not considering that it could have been merged into an existing window.\n\nActually, I think I finally see the bug! Let me look at the code flow again:\n\nFor merging windows:\n1. For each assigned window:\n   a. Call addWindow (which merges with existing windows)\n   b. Check if the resulting window is late\n   c. If late, retire the window and continue\n   d. If not late, add element and set isSkippedElement = false\n\n2. At the end:\n   a. If isSkippedElement && isElementLate, sideoutput\n\nSo the issue is: if an element is assigned to a window that results in a late merged window, we drop the element entirely. But the element might have been valid for one of the pre-existing windows if we hadn't merged them!\n\nWait no, that doesn't make sense. Merging is automatic, we can't avoid it.\n\nOK I think I need to look at this from a different angle. Let me look at the `isWindowLate` and `isElementLate` methods more carefully, and also understand what \"late\" means in the context of event-time windows.\n</thinking>\n\n<function_calls>\n<invoke name=\"mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file\">\n<parameter name=\"repo\">github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc",
  "endLine": 625,
  "path": "flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java",
  "startLine": 605
}
Output
"InputValidationError: [\n  {\n    \"code\": \"unrecognized_keys\",\n    \"keys\": [\n      \"path\",\n      \"startLine\",\n      \"endLine\"\n    ],\n    \"path\": [],\n    \"message\": \"Unrecognized keys: \\\"path\\\", \\\"startLine\\\", \\\"endLine\\\"\"\n  }\n]"
